item.title: SpaceX, Newly Public, to Acquire Cursor for $60 Billion in SpaceX Funny-Money Stock 摘要
item.description:
SpaceX, newly public, agreed to acquire Cursor for $60 billion in stock, representing a 3.4% dilution at SpaceX's IPO valuation. Cursor had crossed $1 billion in annualized revenue in November and was ranked No. 37 on the CNBC Disruptor 50 list in 2026. SpaceX shares rose 16% on Tuesday, making it the fourth most valuable US company by market cap. However, the author criticizes the valuation as insane, noting SpaceX is not profitable and Cursor's $1 billion sales do not justify a 60x revenue multiple, especially since Cursor is also unprofitable.
标签: SpaceX, Cursor, acquisition, valuation, stock, IPO, tech, finance, unprofitable, market cap

item.title: NetNewsWire Status 摘要
item.description:
Brent Simmons reports on a year of intensive work on NetNewsWire, his open-source RSS reader, during retirement. With 2,188 commits, the team modernized the codebase, paid down tech debt, fixed bugs, and improved the foundation before adding new features. The app, already indispensable, is now significantly better and improving rapidly.
标签: NetNewsWire, RSS, open source, software development, tech debt, retirement, app development, modernization, bug fixes

item.title: New Domain for Sign In With Apple and iCloud+ Hide My Email 摘要
item.description:
Apple will unify the email domains for Sign in with Apple and iCloud+ Hide My Email under a single domain, private.icloud.com, later this summer. New addresses will use this domain, while existing addresses on legacy domains (privaterelay.appleid.com and icloud.com) will continue to work. Some commentators worry that services might block the new domain, rendering the feature less effective, but the author argues that such services are likely invasive and not worth using.
标签: Apple, privacy, email, iCloud, Sign in with Apple, Hide My Email, domain change, security

item.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm 摘要
item.description:
The Popa botnet, linked to the Vo1d malware campaign, has infected millions of Android TV boxes to create a residential proxy network for advertising fraud, account takeovers, and data scraping. Researchers from Qurium, Synthient, and Spur connect Popa to NetNut, a proxy service owned by Alarum Technologies. NetNut's SDK, originally developed by Moishi Kramer's Ninjatech, is used without user consent in many cases. Despite Alarum's denial, evidence shows Popa devices forward traffic for NetNut clients, with up to 2.5 million IPs daily. The botnet persists through resellers with minimal KYC checks.
标签: botnet, Android, TV boxes, residential proxy, ad fraud, NetNut, Alarum Technologies, Vo1d, cybersecurity, malware

item.title: Cotypist – Smart Autocomplete Utility for Mac 摘要
item.description:
Cotypist is an AI-powered autocomplete utility for Mac that uses on-device models to suggest words ahead of the insertion point, accepted by pressing Tab. It respects privacy, stores data locally, and integrates via macOS accessibility APIs. The author finds the suggestions eerily good but personally dislikes the experience, feeling it distracts from the joy of writing. However, they acknowledge its potential value for those who find writing laborious, handle repetitive emails, or write in English as a second language. A free tier (100 words/day) and paid tiers ($6-$9/month) are available, with a 30-day Pro trial for new installations.
标签: AI, autocomplete, Mac, writing, productivity, privacy, on-device, accessibility, review, utility
